name: data-platform-helpers
documentation:
PIP Package for a variety of Data Platform Helpers

Table of Contents:
1. Version Checker


### 1. Version Checker
#### Purpose: 
used to determine if a relevant version attribute across related applications are valid.

There are many potential applications for this. Here are a few examples:
1. in a sharded cluster where is is important that the shards and cluster manager have the same
components.
2. kafka connect and kafka broker apps working together and needing to have the same ubnderlying
version.

#### How to use:

0. add to requirements 
    in `requirements.txt`:

    ```
        data-platform-helpers==0.1.1
    ```
1. in `src/charm.py` of requirer + provider

    in constructor [REQUIRED]:

    ```
    from data_platform_helpers.version_check import CrossAppVersionChecker
    ...
    self.version_checker = self.CrossAppVersionChecker(
        self,
        version=x, # can be a revision of a charm, version of a snap, version of a workload, etc
        relations_to_check=[x,y,z],
        # only use if the version doesn't not need to exactly match our current version
        version_validity_range={"x": "<a,>b"})
    ```

    in update status hook [OPTIONAL]:

    ```
    if not self.version_checker.are_related_apps_valid():
        logger.debug(
            "Warning relational version check failed, these relations have mismatched versions",
            "%s",
            self.version_checker(self.version_checker.get_invalid_versions())
        )
        # do something, ie set status, instruct user to change something, etc
    ```

2. other areas of the charm (i.e. joined events, action events, etc) [OPTIONAL]:
    ```
    if not self.charm.version_checker.are_related_apps_valid():
        # do something - i.e. fail event or log message
    ```
3. in upgrade handler of requirer + provider [REQUIRED]:
    ```
    if [last unit to upgrade]:
        self.charm.version.set_version_across_all_relations()
    ```

functions.name: data_platform_helpers.tests_helpers.vms.ha_helpers.cut_network_from_unit_with_ip_change

Cut network from a lxc container in a way the changes the IP.

Args:
    machine_name: lxc container hostname

functions.name: data_platform_helpers.tests_helpers.vms.ha_helpers.cut_network_from_unit_without_ip_change
documentation: Cut network from a lxc container (without causing the change of the unit IP address).

functions.name: data_platform_helpers.tests_helpers.vms.ha_helpers.restore_network_for_unit_with_ip_change

functions.name: data_platform_helpers.tests_helpers.vms.ha_helpers.restore_network_for_unit_without_ip_change
documentation: Restore network from a lxc container (without causing the change of the unit IP address).

modules.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check
documentation:
Class used to determine if a relevant version attribute across related applications are valid.

There are many potential applications for this. Here are a few examples:
1. in a sharded cluster where is is important that the shards and cluster manager have the same
components.
2. kafka connect and kafka broker apps working together and needing to have the same ubnderlying
version.

How to use:

1. in src/charm.py of requirer + provider
    in constructor [REQUIRED]:
    self.version_checker = self.CrossAppVersionChecker(
        self,
        version=x, # can be a revision of a charm, version of a snap, version of a workload, etc
        relations_to_check=[x,y,z],
        # only use if the version doesn't not need to exactly match our current version
        version_validity_range={"x": "<a,>b"})

    in update status hook [OPTIONAL]:
    if not self.version_checker.are_related_apps_valid():
        logger.debug(
            "Warning relational version check failed, these relations have mismatched versions",
            "%s",
            self.version_checker(self.version_checker.get_invalid_versions())
        )
        # can set status, instruct user to change

2. other areas of the charm (i.e. joined events, action events, etc) [OPTIONAL]:
    if not self.charm.version_checker.are_related_apps_valid():
        # do something - i.e. fail event or log message

3. in upgrade handler of requirer + provider [REQUIRED]:
    if [last unit to upgrade]:
        self.charm.version.set_version_across_all_relations()

functions.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.get_charm_revision
documentation:
Returns the charm revision.

TODO: Keep this until ops framework supports: https://github.com/canonical/operator/issues/1255

classes.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.__init__
documentation:
Constructor for CrossAppVersionChecker.

Args:
    charm: charm to inherit from
    version: (int), the current version of the desired attribute of the charm
    relations_to_check: (List), a list of relations who should have compatible versions
        with the current charm
    version_validity_range: (Optional Dict), a list of ranges for valid version ranges.
        If not provided it is assumed that relations on the provided interface must have
        the same version.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.get_invalid_versions
documentation:
Returns a list of (app name, version number) pairs, if the version number mismatches.

Mismatches are decided based on version_validity_range, if version_validity_range is not
provided, then the mismatch is expected to match this current app's version number.

Raises:
    NoVersionError.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.get_version_of_related_app
documentation:
Returns a int for the version of the related app.

Raises:
    NoVersionError.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.get_deployment_prefix
documentation:
Returns the deployment prefix, indicating if the charm is locally deployred or not.

TODO: Keep this until ops framework supports:
    https://github.com/canonical/operator/issues/1255

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.are_related_apps_valid
documentation:
Returns True if a related app has a version that's incompatible with the current app.

Raises:
    NoVersionError.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.set_version_across_all_relations
documentation: Sets the version number across all related apps, prvided by relations_to_check.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.set_version_on_related_app
documentation: Sets the version number across for a specified relation on a specified app.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.set_version_on_relation_created
documentation:
Shares the charm's revision to the newly integrated application.

Raises:
    RelationInvalidError

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.is_integrated_to_locally_built_charm
documentation:
Returns a boolean value indicating whether the charm is integrated is a local charm.

NOTE: this function ONLY checks relations on the provided interfaces in
relations_to_check.

methods.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionChecker.is_local_charm
documentation: Returns a boolean value indicating whether the provided app is a local charm.
Verifies versions across multiple integrated applications.

classes.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.CrossAppVersionCheckerError
documentation: Parent class for errors raised in CrossAppVersionChecker class.

classes.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.RelationInvalidError
documentation: Raised if a relation is not in the provided set of relations to check.

classes.name: data_platform_helpers.version_check.NoVersionError
documentation: Raised if an application does not contain any version information.
